questions.title: Should the city implement measures to make housing more affordable for young couples?
questions.description: This question addresses concerns about the high cost of rent affecting young couples' ability to find affordable housing.
argumentsFor: Making housing more affordable can help young couples establish stable homes and contribute to community growth.
Affordable rent can reduce financial stress and improve quality of life for young families starting out.
argumentsAgainst: Implementing affordability measures may increase taxes or reduce investment in housing development.
Rent controls or subsidies could discourage landlords from maintaining or improving rental properties.

questions.title: Should the city implement measures to reduce rent prices for young couples?
questions.description: This question addresses concerns about the affordability of rent for young couples in the community.
argumentsFor: Lower rent would make housing more accessible for young couples starting their lives together.
Reducing rent can help retain young residents and support local economic growth.
argumentsAgainst: Rent control measures could discourage property investment and reduce housing availability.
Intervening in rent prices might lead to decreased maintenance and quality of rental properties.

questions.title: Should the city council regulate rent prices to make housing more affordable?
questions.description: This question addresses whether the city council should take action to control rent costs to improve housing affordability.
argumentsFor: Regulating rent can prevent excessive increases and protect tenants from displacement.
Affordable housing supports community stability and economic diversity.
argumentsAgainst: Rent control may discourage landlords from maintaining or investing in properties.
Market-driven rent prices can encourage housing development and availability.

questions.title: Should the city implement measures to reduce rent prices for young couples?
questions.description: This question addresses concerns about the affordability of rental housing for young couples in the community.
argumentsFor: Lower rent prices would make housing more accessible for young couples starting their lives together.
Reducing rent could help retain young residents and support local economic growth.
argumentsAgainst: Rent control measures might discourage landlords from maintaining or investing in rental properties.
Intervening in rent prices could reduce the overall supply of rental housing, leading to shortages.

argumentsFor: Regulating rent can prevent excessive increases and protect tenants from displacement.

questions.title: Should measures be taken to address rising fuel prices linked to the conflict in Iran?
questions.description: This question considers whether actions should be implemented to mitigate the impact of the war in Iran on fuel costs.
argumentsFor: Taking measures could help stabilize fuel prices and reduce economic strain on consumers.
Addressing the issue may prevent further inflation and protect the overall economy.
argumentsAgainst: Intervening could involve costly policies that may not effectively control global fuel price fluctuations.
Focusing on this issue might divert resources from other pressing domestic priorities.

questions.title: Should Israel continue military operations until the current regime is overthrown?
questions.description: This question addresses whether Israel should maintain its military campaign with the goal of regime change.
argumentsFor: Continuing the war could lead to the removal of a hostile regime, potentially increasing regional security.
Sustained military pressure may deter future aggression and protect citizens from ongoing threats.
argumentsAgainst: Prolonged conflict risks significant civilian casualties and humanitarian crises.
Extended warfare may destabilize the region further and hinder prospects for peaceful resolution.

argumentsFor: Lower rent would make housing more accessible for young couples starting their lives together.

argumentsAgainst: Rent control measures might discourage landlords from maintaining or investing in rental properties.

questions.title: Should the city council regulate rent prices to make housing more affordable?
questions.description: This question addresses whether local government should take action to control rental costs to improve housing affordability.
argumentsFor: Regulating rent can prevent excessive increases and protect tenants from displacement.
Affordable housing supports community stability and economic diversity.
argumentsAgainst: Rent control may discourage landlords from maintaining or investing in properties.

questions.title: Should the government take action to address rising fuel prices caused by the conflict in Iran?
questions.description: This question considers whether government intervention is necessary to manage fuel price increases linked to the war in Iran.
argumentsFor: Government action could help stabilize fuel prices and protect consumers from economic hardship.
Intervention may reduce the broader economic impact of the conflict on local communities.
argumentsAgainst: Market forces should determine fuel prices without government interference.
Intervening could lead to unintended economic consequences or increased government spending.

questions.title: Should Israel continue military operations until the current regime is overthrown?
questions.description: This question addresses whether Israel should persist with its military actions aimed at regime change.
argumentsFor: Continuing the war could lead to the removal of a hostile regime, potentially increasing regional security.
Sustained military pressure may deter future aggression and protect citizens from ongoing threats.
argumentsAgainst: Prolonged conflict risks significant civilian casualties and humanitarian crises.
Extended warfare may destabilize the region further and hinder prospects for peaceful resolution.

questions.title: Should Pornhub be legally allowed to operate and provide adult content in Australia?
questions.description: This question addresses whether Pornhub should be permitted to legally offer its adult content services within Australia.
argumentsFor: Allowing Pornhub supports freedom of expression and access to adult content for consenting adults.
Legal operation enables regulation and enforcement of age restrictions and content standards.
argumentsAgainst: Pornhub has faced criticism for hosting non-consensual or illegal content, raising concerns about user safety.
Permitting Pornhub may contribute to increased exposure to explicit material, which some believe harms societal values.
